Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Terminating Sanctions And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-18-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Curt Sigfstead's motion for terminating sanctions is granted. No opposition has been filed. The Court finds that further orders would not result in compliance. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2006 Continued To Jul-26-06 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories And Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; And For Order Compelling Attendance At Oral Deposition; And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-28-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
TELEPHONIC APPEARANCE: Peter A. Hass

NO other APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's motion to compel responses to form interrogatories and request for production of documents and to compel attendance at deposition is granted. No opposition has been filed. Plaintiff is ordered to serve complete verified responses without objection within 15 days of notice of this order. Plaintiff is also ordered to contact counsel for defendant to schedule her deposition. The deposition shall be completed within 30 days of notice of this order. The Court finds that plaintiff failed to respond to written discovery and to appear for deposition. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $577 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn For Order Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories & Demand For Production & Inspection Of Documents & For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-08-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's motion to compel responses to form interrogatories and request for production of documents is ordered off calendar as it was not timely served. CCP Section 1005 requires that motions be served and filed 16 court days prior to the hearing, with five calendar days added if the motion was served by mail. The motion was filed on January 13, 2006 and served by mail. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
